%                      section.summary, section.skills, section.experience,
%                      section.education, section.projects
% PURPOSE: Uses paracol for a two-column layout (ATS reading-order risk).
%          Section headings use non-standard names so they are not detected
%          in extracted text → all section checks warn.
\documentclass[10pt, letterpaper]{article}
\usepackage[top=1.5cm, bottom=1.5cm, left=1.5cm, right=1.5cm]{geometry}
\usepackage{hyperref}
\usepackage{enumitem}
\usepackage{paracol}   % INTENTIONAL ISSUE: paracol → layout.package.paracol WARN

\input{glyphtounicode}
\pdfgentounicode=1

\pagestyle{empty}
\setlength{\parindent}{0pt}

\begin{document}

\begin{center}
    {\Large \textbf{Alice Wang}}\\[4pt]
    Montreal, QC \quad|\quad
    \href{mailto:alice@example.com}{alice@example.com} \quad|\quad
    +1 514-555-0177 \quad|\quad
    \href{https://linkedin.com/in/alicewang}{linkedin.com/in/alicewang}
\end{center}

\columnratio{0.35}
\begin{paracol}{2}

% ── LEFT COLUMN ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
% INTENTIONAL ISSUE: Non-standard section names → section.* WARN
